mano_cua.result_summary: External link arrow icon (::after pseudo-element with display:inline-block) can wrap independently to next line from link text at narrow viewport widths. At 110px forced container width, mano-cua observed 'exampl' on one line and '↗ (son' on the next — arrow separated from link text. Root cause: .link-external class lacks white-space:nowrap. Fix adds white-space:nowrap to .link-external.
mano_cua.last_reasoning: mano-cua confirmed via DevTools: <a> element has display:inline, white-space:normal; ::after pseudo-element has display:inline-block, width:8px, white-space:normal. No nowrap protection means arrow can wrap independently. Verified by injecting 110px container width — arrow appeared on separate line from link text.
